title: hopea bracteata var. penangiana
intro: Hopea bracteata var. penangiana, also known as the Penang ironwood, is a variant of the Hopea bracteata tree species, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Hopea bracteata var. penangiana are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and improve respiratory health, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, where the bark or leaves are steeped in hot water to create a medicinal tea.
